title: Zachary Kaufman on Office Hours
summary: Zachary Kaufman (@zacharykaufman), an academic specializing in political science, public policy, and law, talks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about transitional justice, genocide, and the time democracy failed him.
body: Zachary Kaufman (@zacharykaufman), an academic specializing in political science, public policy, and law, talks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about transitional justice, genocide, and the time democracy failed him. The latest from Belfer See more Gallery 1 of 5 Zachary Kaufman: Why U.S. Ignored Japan's Shocking Human Experimentation 2 of 5 3 of 5 Zachary Kaufman: Rwanda Tribunal Worth the $$$? 4 of 5 Zachary Kaufman: Hierarchy of Evils 5 of 5 Zachary Kaufman: On a Plane, Does Majority Rule? Zachary D. Kaufman, J.D., Ph.D., is an academic specializing in law, political science, and public policy. Dr. Kaufman is a Senior Fellow at Harvard University‘s John F. Kennedy School of Government (in the Carr Center for Human Rights Policy). In addition, he continues to be a Visiting Fellow at both Yale Law School and Yale University‘s Genocide Studies Program as well as a Term Member of the Council on Foreign Relations. From 2016 to 2017, Dr. Kaufman will take a leave of absence from Harvard University to serve in the U.S. government as a Council on Foreign Relations International Affairs Fellow. His most recent book is United States Law and Policy on Transitional Justice: Principles, Politics, and Pragmatics (Oxford University Press, 2016). _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article features an interview with Zachary Kaufman, a senior fellow at Harvard's Carr Center for Human Rights Policy, discussing his expertise in transitional justice, genocide, and democratic failures. Kaufman, who holds positions at Yale Law School and the Genocide Studies Program, outlines his academic focus on law, political science, and public policy, particularly regarding international tribunals and U.S. foreign policy. He references his book on U.S. law and transitional justice, and mentions his upcoming fellowship with the U.S. government. The conversation touches on specific cases like Rwanda and broader themes of conflict resolution, human rights, and the ethical dilemmas in post-conflict justice.
result.main_content_zh: 本文是对哈佛大学卡尔人权政策中心高级研究员扎卡里·考夫曼的访谈，探讨了其在过渡时期司法、种族灭绝和民主失败方面的专业见解。考夫曼同时在耶鲁法学院和种族灭绝研究项目担任访问学者，其学术研究集中于法律、政治学和公共政策，特别是涉及国际法庭和美国外交政策的领域。他提到了自己关于美国法律与过渡时期司法的著作，并提及即将在美国政府开展的 fellowship。对话涉及卢旺达等具体案例，以及冲突解决、人权和冲突后司法中的伦理困境等更广泛的主题。
